items.title: Police release body-worn footage of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham's arrest, lay fresh charges
items.summary: Northern Territory Police have released dramatic body-worn footage of the arrest of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham after the 44-year-old jumped a court dock last week, sparking a five-day manhunt.

items.title: Nant whisky founder Keith Batt to enter months-long trial in the Supreme Court of Tasmania
items.summary: Tasmanian whisky distillery owner Keith Batt is charged with more than 700 criminal offences, ranging from fraud, to stealing, to dishonestly obtaining a financial advantage.

items.title: Traditional owners' $2 million loss as Nathan River Resources collapses 'undermines' promises
items.summary: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

items.title: Beekeepers call for biopesticide known to target varroa mite to come to Australia
items.summary: A new biopesticide could help battle growing resistance to varroa mite treatments in Australia.

items.title: Inpex seeks urgent orders to stop Darwin strike as experts fear impact on gas market could be significant
items.summary: Oil and gas giant Inpex has launched proceedings with the Fair Work Commission seeking urgent orders to stop strike action at its Ichthys LNG facility in Darwin.

items.title: High Court decision on NZYQ could lead to tens of millions of dollars worth of immigration claims
items.summary: There are warnings a High Court ruling allowing a man to sue for false imprisonment over his indefinite immigration detention will lead to more claims running into the tens of millions of dollars.

items.title: José Carreras to play Gabba show with Robbie Williams
items.summary: Revered opera star José Carreras will lead a line-up featuring everyone from Robbie Williams to Sheppard at the Gabba's first concert in eight years.

items.title: CFMEU pressured building and construction commission to 'take action' against certain contractors, inquiry hears
items.summary: Former Queensland Building and Construction Commission (QBCC) licencing manager Graham Easterby told the inquiry the regulator was pressured to target certain contractors by the CFMEU.

items.title: Traveller dies after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in WA
items.summary: One person has died after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in Western Australia's Kimberley region.

items.title: Japan's bear attacks are breaking records and spilling into big cities
items.summary: Bear encounters are rising in Japan, and the problem is not confined to the wilderness.

items.title: Bear tranquilised after roaming suburb in Japan
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Victorian coroner recommends mental fitness requirement for gun owners
items.summary: The coroner made the recommendation as part of an inquiry into the death of a gun owner who failed to self-report an extensive history of mental health and substance use issues when renewing his licence.

items.title: NDIS cuts could create unsafe environments and domestic violence risks, inquiry warned
items.summary: Planned cuts to NDIS community participation budgets could leave participants in unsafe environments, including increasing their risk of domestic violence, advocates have warned.

items.title: Jeremy Rockliff admits failings over Madeleine Ogilvie allegations of misleading parliament
items.summary: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff says he "dropped the ball" regarding the allegations against a former minister, likening it to a "pile of crap" he has to clean up from "time to time".

items.title: Woolworths to offshore hundreds of corporate jobs
items.summary: Supermarket giant Woolworths will begin consultations with potentially affected staff today after announcing plans to shift some operations offshore.

items.title: Cyber attack shuts down two Mackay Sugar mills
items.summary: Cane farmers in the Mackay region were notified early on Wednesday to stop harvesting, after a cyber incident brought sugar milling at two north Queensland mills to a halt.

items.title: Neale Daniher's family and friends, AFL identities gather at MCG for MND campaigner's state funeral
items.summary: Family, friends, members of the AFL community and dignitaries attend the service to remember the AFL legend, MND campaigner and former Australia of the Year.

items.title: Blues star Brian To'o on the verge of breaking 35-year-old Origin try-scoring record
items.summary: Brian To'o is one of the best New South Wales Origin players in recent years and now history beckons for the Blues winger in Game II at the MCG.

items.title: Anthony Albanese 'very worried' about human and global economic impacts of Iran war escalation
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says he is "very worried" about fresh US strikes on Iran and has highlighted the difficulties for global markets responding to US President Donald Trump's repeated peace deal announcements.

items.title: Former FIFA official says profit being prioritised over fans at World Cup
items.summary: A former FIFA official says the upcoming World Cup has been tainted by corporate greed as two US states investigate ticket pricing and practices.

items.title: Domestic violence escalated during Hayley's pregnancy — a time when women are at greater risk
items.summary: Pregnancy is a time of greater autonomy and self-awareness for women. Because domestic violence is about power and control, a pregnancy can challenge that for a person who is using abuse.

items.title: Court dismisses appeal to increase jail sentence for killer driver Christopher Dillon Joannidis
items.summary: The Court of Appeal has dismissed an appeal to increase the jail term of Christopher Dillon Joannidis, who killed five people in a horrific 2023 crash in Northern Victoria.

items.title: Mark Latham to pay MP Alex Greenwich $140k as appeal over homophobic tweet judgement fails
items.summary: A Federal Court judge has dismissed an appeal against a judgement which ordered New South Wales MP Mark Latham to pay $140,000 to independent MP Alex Greenwich over a homophobic tweet in 2023.

items.title: Aussie magic in Oakland drives Socceroos at FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Socceroos captain Mat Ryan is a long way from home, but he has found an unexpected connection in Oakland.

items.title: China's Zhuque-2E Y6 rocket launches two new satellites into orbit
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Omar Artan returns home after being denied entry to United States ahead of FIFA World Cup
items.summary: Omar Artan thanks FIFA for its support after he is barred from entering the United States, ending his dream of refereeing at his first World Cup.

items.title: Mystery blue streak spotted over southern Queensland
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Man charged with murder over stabbing of Brandon Yates in Wollongong
items.summary: Police have charged a man and say they expect more arrests in relation to the stabbing of Brandon Yates, who was found fatally injured in an apartment in Wollongong in 2025.

items.title: Elon Musk's xAI hit with class action over data centre power plants causing 'inescapable' noise
items.summary: US residents say a plant powering data centres has caused them emotional distress and reduced the value of their properties.

items.title: Cameron McEvoy says 50m world record just the beginning, Australian Swimming Trials first chance to go faster
items.summary: When Cameron McEvoy broke the long-standing 50m freestyle world record in March, he was at great pains to note that he believes he can go even faster. The Australian Swimming Trials offers the first chance to do so.

items.title: Emergency clean-up ordered at Biloela's Essential Spill
items.summary: Drums and containers of chemical and hazardous materials have been leaking at an abandoned central Queensland premises, flowing into stormwater drains.

items.title: Gippsland croquet player attempts to break world record at 101 years old
items.summary: Gippsland's May Beveridge still plays croquet every week. The 101-year-old may soon be crowned the world's oldest female competitive player.

items.title: Nick Kyrgios wins opening match in Stuttgart after five-month absence from ATP Tour
items.summary: Nick Kyrgios makes a successful return to the court, claiming only his second singles victory since 2022.

items.title: Trump threatens new US strikes, saying Iran's 'taken too long' to make deal - as it happened
items.summary: Donald Trump has threatened to strike Iran's power plants and bridges, saying they 'have to pay the price' for taking too long to negotiate.

items.title: Sugarcane industry worries about future as Cairns's urban growth encroaches
items.summary: Sugarcane growers near Cairns say urban expansion is reducing farmland and putting the region's sugar industry at risk.

items.title: NASA unveils Artemis III astronauts to test technology for future moon landing
items.summary: NASA has revealed the crew for its Artemis III mission, the next step in the US space agency's plan to eventually land astronauts on the moon.

items.title: NSW and federal government tension over hospital bed backlog — as it happened
items.summary: NSW Health Minister Ryan Park has aired his frustrations over the issue of people stuck in hospital beds waiting for a spot in aged care or an NDIS placement.

items.title: Chinese-launched rocket lights up Australian skies as it heads for orbit
items.summary: Some skywatchers describe a silvery or blue-tinted ball of gas that gradually turned red and disappeared.

items.title: Mould, overcrowding and housing delays affecting families in Wilcannia
items.summary: Delays in maintenance repairs, including mould removal, are forcing families to sleep on mattresses in living rooms, adding to overcrowding pressures in remote homes.

items.title: Serena Williams makes winning return to court at Queen's in doubles match
items.summary: Serena Williams steals the show at Queen's Club, as the American icon marks her eagerly awaited return to competitive action with a doubles victory nearly four years after her last match.

items.title: Australia records first ODI loss to Bangladesh in 21 years as hosts win by 86 runs
items.summary: Bangladesh posts just its second victory over Australia in 23 ODIs, recording an 86-run win in the opening match of the series in Dhaka.

items.title: Call for security boost at shopping centres to clamp down on knife risk
items.summary: A retail worker at Sydney's Broadway Shopping Centre says she was left scared after a man told her he had a knife.

items.title: Xi Jinping's North Korea visit is unusual, and shows his need to court Kim Jong Un
items.summary: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un's bilateral talks in North Korea were a big departure from the Chinese president's usual diplomacy playbook because, for this first time this year, he was the one travelling abroad to make the meeting happen.

items.title: Gary Ablett Jr, John Worsfold among Australian Football Hall of Fame inductees
items.summary: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

items.title: Bus overcrowding prompts push for more Brisbane transit lanes
items.summary: Buses are Queensland's most popular form of public transport, but advocates say a lack of investment means they are often unreliable and overcrowded.

items.title: Palestinian father vows to fight for justice for seven-month-old baby killed by Israeli troops
items.summary: A Palestinian father promises to fight for justice for his seven-month-old son, who was shot dead by an Israeli soldier in the West Bank city of Hebron.

items.title: Melbourne councils say state government task force to crack down on illegal dumping is ineffective
items.summary: Councils across Melbourne's suburbs are struggling to combat the rise of illegal dumping, despite over $21 million of funding from the Victorian state government.

items.title: Some countries are protecting a child's right to play — not Australia
items.summary: The first time Renae Powell waved her eldest child through the primary school gates she felt something was missing: play time.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: When does it start, who are the favourites, what time do the Socceroos play?
items.summary: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

items.title: ABC is looking for storytellers for International Day of People with Disability
items.summary: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

items.title: Fuel shock spurs business uptake of green equipment including batteries and EVs
items.summary: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

items.title: How Rolf Harris hid in plain sight
items.summary: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

items.title: Travel quiz, June 3: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off on a trip
items.summary: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

items.title: Trump vows to respond after Iran shoots down helicopter patrolling Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: US President Donald Trump accuses Iran of shooting down a US Apache helicopter that was patrolling the Strait of Hormuz, vowing to retaliate.

items.title: Eight killed in south Lebanon strike as Israel warns entire city to evacuate
items.summary: For the first time, Israel's military has warned the entire city of Tyre to evacuate, causing thousands of people to flee.

items.title: Northern Ireland police appeal for calm after asylum seeker arrested over Belfast stabbing
items.summary: Protesters hold anti-immigration demonstrations in Northern Ireland and England after a Sudanese man is arrested over a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

items.title: Sienna Toohey makes Commonwealth Games swimming team after move to Canberra
items.summary: Seventeen-year-old Sienna Toohey says qualifying for the Commonwealth Games makes moving away from friends and family to Canberra worth it.

items.title: Tens of thousands displaced after magnitude-7.8 earthquake in Philippines
items.summary: Rescuers are combing through collapsed buildings in the southern Philippines after a powerful earthquake devastated coastal communities.

items.title: Palestinians 'trapped' between Israeli forces, settlers and Hamas, UN report finds
items.summary: A UN report finds that Palestinians are being subjected to "mass atrocities" by Israeli forces and being "violently repressed and controlled by the very faction that claims to govern them".

items.title: Teens recruited by crime syndicates for arson attacks
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Encrypted apps including Signal and Discord used by criminals to lure teens with intellectual disabilities into crime
items.summary: Criminal syndicates are targeting vulnerable children with no criminal history, low IQ and intellectual disabilities to carry out their dirty work through encrypted apps.

items.title: Priscilla Hon calls out prize money disparity at Libéma Open tennis tournament
items.summary: Priscilla Hon draws attention to the difference between the men's and women's winners' prize money at the Libéma Open in The Netherlands.

items.title: Confidence assured in Macquarie Point Stadium delivery at meeting of AFL presidents
items.summary: Despite suggestions that potential delays and cost blowouts of Tasmania's new stadium may be on the minds of AFL heads, the topic was barely mentioned at the presidents' annual meeting.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Disney on Ice spoiled the sport's finals plans, so how does it secure better venues?
items.summary: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

items.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament
items.summary: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

items.title: Man jailed for running over and seriously injuring SA Police officer
items.summary: A man, who was on the run from authorities in Victoria, has been jailed for eight years after mowing down a police officer in South Australia.
